food_subgroup: Pizza combo meal with soft drinks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn code exists for a bundled 2-pizza combo meal with soft drinks. FOODON_00002682 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW (Pizza) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a bundled multi-pizza combo with beverages.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for a commercial pizza combo bundle product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a full 2-pizza combo bundle comprising two individual pizzas (approximately 350–400 g each) and two soft drinks (approximately 330 ml each), as sold at Now Pizza, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, regular crust (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #21299) and Carbonated beverage, cola (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14400); weighted composite estimate for 2-pizza combo with soft drinks
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutritional data exists for Now Pizza's '2 Way' combo. Values are estimated as a weighted composite: approximately 80% pizza (cheese, regular crust, USDA SR Legacy #21299: 266 kcal, 11.4 g protein, 9.8 g fat, 33.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g) and approximately 20% cola soft drink (USDA SR Legacy #14400: 37 kcal, 0 g protein, 0 g fat, 9.6 g carbohydrate per 100 g). Weighted per-100 g composite: energy ~220 kcal, protein ~9.1 g, fat ~7.8 g, carbohydrate ~26.8 g. Sodium estimated from USDA pizza data (~560 mg/100 g pizza component, blended). Saturated fat, sugar, and fibre similarly blended. This is a category-level estimate; actual values depend on pizza toppings chosen.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI ~60); Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. Am J Clin Nutr. 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Now Pizza's '2 Way' combo. GI of 60 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza (regular/thin crust), which is the closest analogous food. The soft drink component (cola) has a GI of approximately 63 (University of Sydney GI Database). The blended combo GI is estimated at ~60 (medium), dominated by the pizza component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 241.2) / 100 ≈ 145 g, where carbohydrate per 900 g serving = 26.8 × 9 = 241.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~450 mg/100 g; ~4050 mg per full combo serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this combo meal, as a single serving may provide approximately 175% of the recommended daily sodium intake of 2300 mg, primarily from pizza dough, cheese, and processed toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~145 per full combo serving) and significant refined carbohydrate content from pizza dough and cola soft drinks
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this combo meal due to its very high glycemic load per serving driven by refined wheat dough and sugar-sweetened soft drinks, which will cause a substantial postprandial blood glucose rise; if consumed, portion size must be strictly limited and paired with low-GI foods.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~3.5 g/100 g), high sodium (~450 mg/100 g), and high caloric density (~220 kcal/100 g) across a large serving
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this combo as an occasional indulgence only, as the combination of saturated fat from cheese, high sodium from processed toppings and dough, and excess calories from both pizza and cola soft drinks collectively elevates cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The '2 Way' is a pizza combo meal sold by Now Pizza at their Funan outlet in Singapore. The bundle consists of two pizzas — allowing the customer to mix and match flavours — accompanied by two soft drinks (carbonated cola-type beverages). The pizzas are baked with a regular wheat-flour crust, tomato-based sauce, mozzarella cheese, and variable toppings depending on customer selection. The combo is designed for sharing between two people or as a value meal for one hungry diner. Nutritionally, the combo is energy-dense, providing approximately 220 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from cheese and toppings (~9 g/100 g) and moderate fat (~7.8 g/100 g, of which ~3.5 g is saturated). The carbohydrate content (~26.8 g/100 g) is dominated by refined wheat starch from the crust and added sugars from the soft drinks. Sodium is notably high (~450 mg/100 g), making the full combo a significant sodium load. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 60 (medium), but the glycemic load per full serving is very high (~145) due to the large portion size. This meal is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without strict portion control. It is a processed, Western-style fast food product positioned in the Singapore quick-service restaurant market.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional data exists for Now Pizza's '2 Way' combo product. Values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for generic cheese pizza and cola soft drink, weighted by approximate serving composition. Actual nutrient values will vary significantly depending on pizza flavours selected, crust thickness, and topping quantities. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cheese pizza (#21299) and cola soft drink (#14400), blended at approximately 80:20 ratio by weight. Actual values depend on pizza flavour selection. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy pizza source entry and is not expected to be significant in this product. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this food category. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this food category. 3. GI of 60 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database cheese pizza entry; the full combo glycemic load is very high (~145) due to large serving size. 4. Halal status is listed as false/unknown as Now Pizza's halal certification status could not be confirmed from approved sources at time of processing. 5. The food_name has been normalised from the promotional label '2 Way' to the canonical descriptive English name 'Pizza Combo (2-Pizza Set with Soft Drinks)' to satisfy Q1 food identity requirements. 6. Multilingual names are provided per Singapore cuisine rules (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as this is a Singapore-based restaurant product. 7. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy pizza data and are approximate. 8. Serving size of 900 g is an estimate for the full combo (2 × ~350 g pizza + 2 × ~100 g cola equivalent by weight contribution).
